title: A strange sound in my building in Belgrade
summary: A short story about a sound that bothered you for weeks until you finally found out what it was.
topic: A strange sound in my building in Belgrade
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a strange sound in your home or building that you finally understood. Describe when it happened, what you thought it was, and what it really was. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: For two weeks after I moved into…, I kept hearing a strange sound at…
At first I thought it was…
sampleText: For two weeks after I moved into my apartment in Vračar, Belgrade, I kept hearing a strange sound at almost exactly five in the morning. It was a slow, soft thump-thump-thump from above me, like a heavy heart beating through the ceiling. The first night I thought it was a pipe. The second night I thought it was someone exercising. By the fourth night, I was lying in bed at four forty-five with my eyes wide open, waiting for it to start. I asked the woman in apartment 12 about it, but she only shrugged and said, 'It's an old building.' One Saturday morning I finally walked up to the fifth floor and knocked on the door. An elderly woman opened it, wearing a flour-covered apron. Behind her, I could see a wooden table and a long pin of dough, which she was rolling out for that day's bread. Every morning, before the city woke up, she made bread for her three grandchildren who came for breakfast. I felt extremely embarrassed for ever thinking it was a ghost. After that, the thump-thump-thump above my head sounded like a small, warm clock. I still listened for it every morning until the day I moved out.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I kept hearing a strange sound
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi cứ nghe một âm thanh kỳ lạ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a slow, soft thump-thump-thump from above me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một tiếng thịch-thịch-thịch chậm rãi, nhẹ nhàng từ phía trên

sampleAnnotations.phrase: like a heavy heart beating through the ceiling
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: như một trái tim nặng đập qua trần nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was lying in bed at four forty-five
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang nằm trên giường lúc bốn giờ bốn mươi lăm
Đây là một tòa nhà cũ
mặc một chiếc tạp dề dính bột

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she was rolling out for that day's bread
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bà đang cán cho ổ bánh mì hôm đó
trước khi thành phố thức giấc
Tôi cảm thấy cực kỳ xấu hổ
một chiếc đồng hồ nhỏ, ấm áp

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Kept + -ing' for repeated past action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I kept hearing a strange sound' dùng 'keep + V-ing' để nhấn mạnh sự lặp lại của một hành động trong quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I kept hearing a strange sound' uses 'keep + -ing' to highlight repetition of an action in the past.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple in 'while'-style scenes
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was lying in bed... waiting for it to start' và 'she was rolling out' đều là past continuous mô tả tình huống đang diễn ra; past simple ('knocked', 'opened') đánh dấu các sự kiện cụ thể trong tình huống đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was lying in bed... waiting for it to start' and 'she was rolling out' are past continuous for ongoing situations; past simple ('knocked', 'opened') marks specific events inside them.

sampleTranslationVi: Trong hai tuần sau khi tôi chuyển vào căn hộ ở Vračar, Belgrade, tôi cứ nghe một âm thanh kỳ lạ vào gần đúng năm giờ sáng. Đó là một tiếng thịch-thịch-thịch chậm rãi, nhẹ nhàng từ phía trên, như một trái tim nặng đập qua trần nhà. Đêm đầu tiên tôi nghĩ đó là một đường ống. Đêm thứ hai tôi nghĩ đó là ai đó tập thể dục. Đến đêm thứ tư, tôi đang nằm trên giường lúc bốn giờ bốn mươi lăm với đôi mắt mở to, chờ nó bắt đầu. Tôi hỏi người phụ nữ ở căn hộ 12 về điều đó, nhưng cô ấy chỉ nhún vai và nói, 'Đây là một tòa nhà cũ.' Một sáng thứ Bảy, tôi cuối cùng đi lên tầng năm và gõ cửa. Một bà cụ mở cửa, mặc một chiếc tạp dề dính bột. Phía sau bà, tôi có thể thấy một chiếc bàn gỗ và một cây cán dài bột, mà bà đang cán cho ổ bánh mì hôm đó. Mỗi sáng, trước khi thành phố thức giấc, bà làm bánh mì cho ba đứa cháu đến ăn sáng. Tôi cảm thấy cực kỳ xấu hổ vì từng nghĩ đó là ma. Sau đó, tiếng thịch-thịch-thịch phía trên đầu tôi nghe như một chiếc đồng hồ nhỏ, ấm áp. Tôi vẫn lắng nghe nó mỗi sáng cho đến ngày tôi chuyển đi.

options: I kept hearing a strange sound at five in the morning.
I kept to hear a strange sound at five in the morning.
I kept hear a strange sound at five in the morning.
I have kept hear a strange sound at five in the morning.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past continuous sentence.
options: I lying in bed at four forty-five with my eyes wide open.
I was lying in bed at four forty-five with my eyes wide open.
I lay in bed at four forty-five with my eyes wide open.
I have lain in bed at four forty-five with my eyes wide open.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was lying' for an ongoing past situation.

options: a sharp metallic sound
a high musical note
a dull heavy sound, like something soft hitting a surface
the sound of running water

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct phrasal verb sentence.
options: I knocked at door.
I knocked to the door.
I knocked in the door.
I knocked on the door.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Knock on (the) door' is the standard collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'turn out'.
options: It turned out that she made bread every morning.
It turned out what she made bread every morning.
It is turned out that she made bread every morning.
It turn out that she made bread every morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'It turned out that + clause' = it was eventually revealed that.

options: feeling very excited and happy
feeling uncomfortable because of a mistake or awkward thought
feeling hungry late at night
feeling cold

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past continuous sentence about action in progress.
options: She rolling out the dough for that day's bread.
She rolled out the dough for that day's bread.
She was rolling out the dough for that day's bread.
She is rolling out the dough for that day's bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was rolling' for an action in progress at a past moment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'before + clause'.
options: Before the city wakes up, she made bread.
Before the city was wake up, she made bread.
Before the city is woken up, she made bread.
Before the city woke up, she made bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'woke up' inside a 'before' clause about a past habit.

options: to raise your shoulders to show that you do not know or do not care
to nod your head firmly
to clap your hands once
to bow politely
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Shrug' = the shoulder gesture of uncertainty or indifference.

options: I felt extreme embarrassed.
I felt extremely embarrassed.
I am felt extremely embarrass.
I felted extremely embarrass.
I still listen for it every morning until the day I moved out.
I still listening for it every morning until the day I moved out.
I still listened for it every morning until the day I moved out.
I am still listened for it every morning until the day I moved out.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'listened' fits the past timeframe ending at 'moved out'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why is a sound scary when you don't know its source, but comforting once you do? What does that say about the way fear works?
